title: Piezoelectric energy harvesting system
abstract: The disclosed energy harvesting system (100) comprises a bendable metal plate (12) connected to a piezoelectric plate (11). A pocket (HI) is formed at a central part (12c) of the metal plate opposite the piezoelectric plate. A force receiving surface (31) moves under influence of an applied external force (Fe), while a pressure centralizing element (33) is configured to focus a force (FI) exclusively to a center (11c) of the piezoelectric plate. The metal plate thus bends to a compressive bending position under the influence of the external force by a combination of the central force (FI) and edge forces (F2) on the outer edges of the metal plate. A resilient element (32) laterally surrounds the piezoelectric plate, the metal plate, and the pressure centralizing element, while providing more resistance beyond a threshold configured to protect the piezoelectric plate against damage from excessive compression and/or bending.
claims:
Claims 1. An energy harvesting system (100) comprising - a first piezo-electric plate (11) configured to output electrical energy (E) to an electrical circuit (40) under influence of compressive forces (Fc) changing the first piezo-electric plate (11) between an uncompressed state (CO) and a compressed state (Cl); - a bendable first metal plate (12) connected to the first piezo electric plate (11) for applying the compressive forces (Fc) on the first piezo electric plate (11) when bending the first metal plate (12) from an unbent position (B0) to a compressive bending position (Bl) with outer edges (12e) of the first metal plate (12) bending towards and partially around the first piezo-electric plate (11); - a first pocket structure (13) forming a first pocket (Hi) disposed at a central part (12c) of the first metal plate (12) opposite the first piezo electric plate (11) for allowing the bending of the first metal plate (12) at least partly into the first pocket (Hi) in the compressive bending position (Bl), wherein the first pocket structure (13) is configured to abut the first metal plate (12) exclusively at its outer edges (12e) with edge forces (F2) of the abutting first pocket structure (13) on the first metal plate (12) directed towards the first piezo-electric plate (11); - a force receiving surface (31) configured to move under influence of an external force (Fe), applied to the force receiving surface (31), from an equilibrium surface position (SO) to a compressed surface position (Si) closer towards the first pocket structure (13); - a pressure centralizing element (33) disposed centrally with respect to the first piezo-electric plate (11) between the force receiving surface (31) and the first piezo-electric plate (11), and configured to focus a central force (Fl) exclusively to a center (11c) of the first piezo-electric plate (11) towards the first piezo -electric plate (11) upon application of the 

 external force (Fe) moving the force receiving surface (31) to the compressed surface position (Si); and - a resilient element (32) which in plane surrounds a stack of the first piezo-electric plate (11), the first metal plate (12), and the pressure centralizing element (33); wherein the first metal plate (12) is configured to bend to the compressive bending position (Bl) under the influence of the external force (Fe) by a combination of the central force (Fl) on the center (11c) of the first piezo-electric plate (11) and the edge forces (F2), oppositely directed to the central force (Fl), on the outer edges (12e) of the first metal plate (12), wherein the resilient element (32) is adapted to provide relatively easy compression by the external force (Fe) over an initial range, while providing more resistance beyond a threshold, wherein the threshold is configured to protect the first piezo-electric plate (11) against damage from excessive compression and/or bending. 2. The system (100) according to claim 1, comprising - a second piezo-electric plate (21) and a bendable second metal plate (22) connected to the second piezo-electric plate (21) for applying compressive forces (Fc) on the second piezo-electric plate (21) when bent and outputting electrical energy to the electrical circuit; - a second pocket structure (23) forming a second pocket (H2) at a central part (22c) of the second metal plate (22) opposite the second piezo electric plate (21) for allowing the bending of the second metal plate (22) at least partly into the second pocket (H2), wherein the second pocket structure (23) is configured to abut the second metal plate (22) exclusively at its outer edges (22e) with edge forces (F2) directed towards the second piezo electric plate (21); - wherein the pressure centralizing element (33) is disposed centrally between the piezo -electric plates (11,21), and configured to focus 

 the central force (Fl) on either side exclusively to the respective centers (11c, 21c) of the piezo -electric plates (11,21) upon application of the external force (Fe) moving the force receiving surface (31) to the compressed surface position (Si). 3. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the resilient element (32) is arranged between the force receiving surface (31) and the first pocket structure (13) for restoring the force receiving surface (31) from the compressed surface position (Si) to the equilibrium surface position (SO) in the absence of external force (Fe). 4. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the resilient element (32) is formed by a compressible side wall foam layer. 5. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first and/or optional second pocket structures (13,23) comprise a respective foil with a hole centralized with respect to a position of the metal plate. 6. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first and optional second piezo-electric plate (11,21) are piezoceramic plates. 7. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the energy harvesting system (100) comprises a rectifier circuit (41) electrically connected to electrodes of the first and/or second piezo-electric plate (11,21). 8. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the energy harvesting system (100) comprises one rectifier circuit (41) per pair of first and second piezo-electric plates (11,21), wherein top electrodes of the piezo-electric plates, opposite the respective metal plates are connected to each other as a first terminal connected to the rectifier circuit (41), and bottom electrodes of the piezo-electric plates at a side of the respective metal plates are connected to each other as a second terminal connected to the rectifier circuit (41) [parallel connection]. 9. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a respective combination of a piezo -electric plate (11) and metal plate (12) is formed by a piezo-electric buzzer. 10. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a diameter of the first and optional second piezo-electric plate (11.21) is larger than a diameter of the pressure centralizing element (33) by at least a factor two. 11. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a diameter of the first and optional second metal plate (12,22) is larger than a diameter of the first and optional second pocket (Hl,H2), respectively. 12. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a diameter of the first and optional second metal plate (12,22) is larger than a diameter of the first and optional second piezo-electric plate (11.21), respectively. 13. The system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein a diameter of the first and optional second piezo-electric plate 

 (11,12) is smaller than a diameter of the first and optional second pocket (Hl,H2), respectively. 14. A floor mat comprising the system (100) according to any of the preceding claims, configured to power an electrical device when a person walks over the floor mat. 15. Use of the system (100) according to any of the preceding claims for harvesting energy (E) from a force receiving surface (31) moving under the influence of external force (Fe)
